title: Standardize dashboard money icons on Coins
description:
Dashboard money, cost, and price iconography should use the lucide Coins icon.

Behavior spec:
Feature: Dashboard money icon consistency
Scenario: A user views a cost or price affordance
Given a dashboard UI element represents money, cost, or price
When an icon is shown for that concept
Then the icon is Coins
And CircleDollarSign is not used because it is US-biased.

Definition of Done:
- Replace remaining CircleDollarSign dashboard usage with Coins.
- Verify no CircleDollarSign imports/usages remain in dashboard source.
- Run focused relevant tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented locally for review. Search showed CircleDollarSign only remained in dashboard/components/ProcedureTask.tsx cost breakdown. Replaced it with lucide Coins and verified no CircleDollarSign/circle-dollar-sign references remain under dashboard. Focused test passed: npm test -- ProcedureTask.optimizer-auth.test.tsx. Leaving uncommitted per review policy.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Follow-up: user clarified the Cost: label was in EvaluationTask, not ProcedureTask. Replaced the EvaluationTask detail cost text label with the Coins icon and added focused coverage asserting the icon renders with accessible label Cost while the literal Cost: text is gone. Verification: npm test -- EvaluationTask.category-filter.test.tsx passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Follow-up: centralized EvaluationTask related-resource timestamps in RelatedResourceCard via rightTimestamp, replacing duplicated inline Timestamp JSX/classes for Procedure and Score Version. Focused test passed: npm test -- EvaluationTask.category-filter.test.tsx. Typecheck attempted but hung with no output after about 90s; killed the stuck Plexus-2 tsc process.
